title: Operator floated per-token encryption as a paid-tier feature for zero-trust situations; placement question is where in the layer stack
content: Operator framed the idea as: a value-added encryption service where out-of-band exchange handles keys and encrypted tokens flow through AMS in-band. Floated as wrappable for zero-trust deployments. The implicit positioning was paid-tier feature, with the architectural placement deliberately left open. The conversation then converged on pre-syndication placement (producer-side wrapper encrypts before the wire) as most secure, since it removes the broker, the StreamBufferDO, and every non-key-holding subscriber from the content trust loop.

title: Initial Claude misclaim about token-count derivation degrading under encryption was operator-corrected; cardinality is 1:1
content: Claude initially listed token-count derivation degradation as a side effect of encryption, asserting that encrypted streams would force observability subscribers to count ciphertext bytes rather than LLM tokens. Operator corrected: if the encryption unit is the wire-token unit (as D0001 already locks), then 1 plaintext token in produces exactly 1 ciphertext token out — frame count preserved, token-count derivation continues to operate without change. Claude had conflated this with byte-tokenizer approximation over arbitrary opaque payloads, which was the wrong cut for AMS since the wire unit was always already the token. Correction adopted; the 1:1 cardinality property is now a load-bearing correctness invariant of P0001.

title: No existing proposals track in canon — proposals would need a new directory and governance doc to land cleanly
content: Searched canon for proposal/governance/status-proposed patterns. Closest hits: docs/homepage-governance.md (governance doc precedent), D0007 (locking-surface discipline at decision level), D0008 (HORIZON as constraint catalog vs. decision catalog), knowledge-base.md (overlay placement). All canon decisions to date use status: active. No directory exists for unsettled material under review. To open P0001 cleanly required bootstrapping the proposals track itself — hence the dual deliverable (docs/proposals-governance.md + canon/proposals/P0001-...).

title: Encryption-unit alignment with wire-unit preserves all frame-count-derived metrics; this is the discipline that lets pre-syndication encryption compose with the rest of the substrate
content: A naïve encryption scheme that buffered N plaintext tokens and emitted them as one larger ciphertext blob would break wire-token semantics, break selective subscription's per-frame routing, and break the buffer's per-token eviction. The 1:1 discipline (encrypt at the wire unit, not below or above it) is what makes pre-syndication encryption compose cleanly with D0017 (selective subscription), D0018 (multi-stream), D0019 (cross-session resume), and D0016 (buffer eviction). This generalizes: any future content-transformation primitive AMS documents must preserve 1:1 frame cardinality or it forfeits clean composition with the existing wire-shape decisions.

title: TLS is structurally unsuitable as the AMS encryption layer despite already running at the transport
content: TLS at wss:// is point-to-point. Each subscriber's connection has its own TLS session; the broker decrypts inbound, broadcasts plaintext within the Worker, re-encrypts outbound to each subscriber. The broker sees plaintext. Adding "another" TLS layer between specific peers does not fix this because peers do not have direct connections — every peer-to-peer path traverses the broker. The trust property the encryption layer exists to deliver (broker outside the content trust loop) requires application-layer encryption above TLS, not at TLS.

title: Naked RSA per token is the wrong altitude in 2026; modern messaging encryption uses RSA only as a key-encapsulation step inside hybrid schemes
content: Three rejection grounds: (i) RSA-OAEP at 2048 bits caps plaintext at ~190 bytes, and tokens routinely exceed this; chaining multiple RSA blocks per token would break 1:1 frame cardinality. (ii) RSA-encrypt per token is orders of magnitude slower than AES-GCM and would dominate emit latency. (iii) Modern messaging primitives (HPKE, MLS, Megolm, libsodium crypto_box) all use RSA — when they use it at all — as a key-encapsulation step inside a hybrid construction. Naked RSA as a per-message primitive is not how 2026 messaging is built. AES-256-GCM with stream-scoped symmetric key (with HPKE as the asymmetric path for sealed-recipient cases) is the right altitude.

title: P0001 commits to pre-syndication placement and AES-256-GCM as v1 primitive; status is PROPOSED until operator answers the D0020 dial-tone-vs-application question
content: Drafted as canon/proposals/P0001-stream-encryption-as-pre-syndication-wrapper.md. Status: proposed. The proposal commits architectural shape (pre-syndication, around the buffer pipeline, at the producer's wrapper), the v1 symmetric primitive (AES-256-GCM with stream-scoped key, 12-byte nonce + ciphertext + 16-byte auth tag, monotonic nonce, stream_id as AAD), HPKE as the documented asymmetric path, MLS and Megolm as deferred future primitives, and the 1:1 frame-cardinality correctness property. The proposal explicitly does NOT commit on the placement question — whether AMS itself ships the wrappers as a paid platform-level VAS (option b, dial-tone) or documents the pattern and leaves implementation to third-party VAS providers (option a, application — D0020's default). Operator answers (a) or (b) at promotion time. Quality 4/5 (not 5/5) because the open question is unresolved by design; promotion will produce the strong-quality DNNNN.

title: A proposals track is bootstrapped at canon/proposals/ with parallel PNNNN numbering, distinct from the DNNNN active-canon series
content: Drafted as docs/proposals-governance.md. The track separates settled decisions (canon/decisions/, status: active, often irreversible) from ideas under review (canon/proposals/, status: proposed). PNNNN numbering is independent of DNNNN; on promotion, a proposal receives a new DNNNN at promotion time and the proposal file is removed in the same atomic PR. Rejected and withdrawn proposals retain their PNNNN with status: rejected or status: withdrawn and a rationale section. Required frontmatter, required content sections, lifecycle states, promotion procedure, and branch/PR conventions are all named. The governance doc itself is status: active because it governs the lifecycle even though everything it manages starts as status: proposed.

title: Any content-transformation primitive AMS documents in the future must preserve 1:1 frame cardinality with the wire-token unit
content: Generalized from the 1:1 cardinality property in P0001. The wire-token unit is fixed by D0001. Pre-syndication encryption preserves it. So must any future primitive that sits between producer-emit and the wire — compression, redaction, signing, framing transformation. If such a primitive collapses or expands frame counts, it forfeits clean composition with selective subscription (D0017), multi-stream (D0018), buffer eviction (D0016), and cross-session resume (D0019). This constraint should be promoted into a tier-2 principle if any future primitive proposal lands.

title: OOB key exchange must remain structurally outside AMS endpoints; keys never traverse the wire
content: Codified in P0001. The producer's wrapper holds the key when emitting; the consumer's wrapper holds the key when decrypting; the wire, the buffer, and any non-key-holding subscriber never hold the key. Mechanism (KMS, separate REST endpoint, capability-bearer-token-with-embedded-key, X3DH-style asynchronous handshake, etc.) is deployment choice. The architectural commitment is "OOB, not via AMS." This keeps the wire's permanent non-goals list intact and ensures that even under platform-shipped placement (option b), AMS never custodies keys.

title: D0020's dial-tone-vs-application test is the gating question for whether P0001 promotes as platform-shipped (option b) or third-party VAS pattern (option a)
content: The default per D0020 is application — third party builds it, AMS documents the pattern, ecosystem fills in. The exception threshold is "foundational components without which the ecosystem cannot form" (TLS-to-HTTPS analogy). The case for crossing the threshold: regulated and enterprise tiers cannot adopt a substrate without a credible first-party zero-trust story; a third-party-only answer is read as "the platform doesn't take security seriously"; AMS forfeits the high-margin segment to a competitor that ships platform-level encryption. The case against: every "AMS should ship it" temptation strengthens lock-in and crowds out ecosystem; the platform's value is the substrate, not the products on top. Operator decides at promotion. Quality 4/5 (not 5/5) because the answer is genuinely unresolved.

title: P0001 and proposals-governance are staged on branch claude/proposals-track-and-P0001-encryption-3K7m; PR not yet opened pending operator review
content: Three files staged at /home/claude/agent-messaging-service on branch claude/proposals-track-and-P0001-encryption-3K7m: docs/proposals-governance.md (status: active, governs the proposals lifecycle), canon/proposals/P0001-stream-encryption-as-pre-syndication-wrapper.md (status: proposed), journal/2026-05-04-stream-encryption-proposal-exploration.tsv (this file). Next step is operator review of (i) the governance shape — whether the proposals track is structured the way the project wants, (ii) the proposal's frontmatter and section coverage, (iii) any edits before opening the PR. PR title convention per the new governance: "canon: open P0001 — stream encryption as a pre-syndication wrapper primitive". Standard PR-independence rule applies — this PR contains the governance + P0001 + journal only, no code or unrelated canon edits.

title: The PAT and Anthropic API key embedded in project instructions remain unrotated; this is a pre-existing security hygiene item unchanged by this work
content: Per project memory and prior session journals, both the GitHub PAT (klappy/klappy.dev + klappy/oddkit, scoped to contents + PRs) and the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY embedded in the project instructions have been flagged as needing rotation across multiple sessions. P0001's work used the existing PAT to clone and stage. No rotation occurred in this session. Standing open item — independent of the encryption proposal but worth re-flagging because the proposal's framing centers on key hygiene and the pre-existing condition is the strongest possible argument for the discipline P0001 codifies.

title: Operator clarified tokens are arbitrary opaque entities, not LLM tokens specifically — Claude's "1:1 LLM-token to wire-token" framing in P0001 v1 was over-specified
content: D0001 fixes the granularity (the wire moves at token shape, not message shape) and the opacity (broker MUST NOT inspect payload per PROTOCOL.md §7) but does NOT fix what one token *means*. The wire-token is whatever the producer chose to emit as one frame — LLM subword token, single character, JSON fragment, binary chunk. Claude's earlier P0001 draft repeatedly framed the cardinality property as "1:1 LLM-token to wire-token" which over-specified what AMS actually commits to. Correction: the cardinality property is "1 plaintext token frame in → 1 ciphertext token frame out" agnostic to producer-defined token semantics. P0001's Cardinality section rewritten to make the tokens-as-arbitrary-entities discipline explicit and to generalize the property to "any future content-transformation primitive must preserve 1:1 frame cardinality."

title: Operator upgraded key rotation from operational concern to primitive-level mandatory property — driven by the patient-adversary and training-corpus exfiltration threat models
content: Claude's P0001 v1 placed forward secrecy in the "not provided by this primitive alone... operational concern... out of v1 scope" bucket. Operator corrected: rotation is mandatory at the primitive level. The reasoning the operator surfaced and Claude fully integrated: AMS streams are exactly the high-value training data future model trainers want (agent reasoning, tool calls, multi-agent negotiation, sensitive workflow content). Without rotation, a single later key compromise retroactively unlocks the entire historical ciphertext corpus. Once that meaning enters a future model's weights via training, it cannot be revoked or unlearned — the damage is permanent and propagates through every derivative model. Mandatory rotation bounds the corpus a single key compromise can unlock. This makes rotation a primitive-level commitment, not deployment guidance. P0001 substantially restructured: new Threat Model section names the patient adversary and training-corpus exfiltration explicitly; new Mandatory Rotation section specifies triggers (1 hour OR 10000 frames OR explicit producer event), mechanism (set_metadata announcement, no new wire feature), and the buffer composition (encryption composes ON TOP of D0016 via configuration discipline — rotation cadence ≤ buffer TTL means the buffer never holds frames whose keys have retired; D0016's exactly-four-property primitive shape is NOT modified).

title: Without mandatory rotation, AMS would be structurally a training-corpus harvester for whoever eventually compromises a key — this generalizes beyond encryption
content: The training-exfiltration threat applies to any system that durably stores high-value structured signal under static keys. The compartmentalization principle (key lifetime bounds corpus exposure to that key's active window) generalizes to any primitive AMS adds that touches durable storage of valuable content. Worth promoting to a tier-2 principle if a second primitive proposal lands with the same property: "compartmentalization of compromise — any durable-storage primitive must bound the corpus a single credential compromise unlocks." Filed as a horizon-shaped observation, not encoded as constraint yet — needs a second instance before crystallizing.

title: Buffer-cannot-outlive-its-key is achieved by composition discipline, NOT by extending D0016's primitive — the encryption layer composes ON TOP of D0016 via deployment configuration
content: Initial P0001 v2 framing claimed key-version became a third eviction bound on D0016 and called this "a friendly extension, not a break." The oddkit_challenge gauntlet (run before PR) flagged this for verification, and a fetch of D0016 §"The Primitive's Shape" confirmed the framing was wrong. D0016 explicitly says: "A buffering primitive has exactly four properties... All other behavior is opaque." Adding a third eviction bound modifies the primitive's shape, which would amend a tier-1 irreversible decision — the opposite of "friendly." Corrected framing: encryption composes ON TOP of D0016 without modifying it. Recommended deployment configures rotation cadence ≤ buffer TTL so frames whose keys retire have already been evicted by TTL; D0016's primitive shape is unchanged. Permitted-but-not-recommended deployments (rotation > TTL) leave consumer-side decryption-failure as the visible degradation, with the buffer remaining pure. Encryption is a wrapper-layer concern; buffering is a primitive concern; the two compose at the configuration layer without entangling at the primitive layer. The "third eviction bound" framing was vodka-violating; this corrected framing is the actual vodka answer.

title: oddkit gauntlet (3 challenges + audit + manual URI check) caught the D0016-extension framing error; P0001 v3 corrects it
content: Three challenges run with mode=canon-tier-1 against load-bearing P0001 claims: (1) mandatory rotation as primitive-level — no hard tensions; gauntlet flagged missing reversibility statement and missing explicit rejected-alternative, both since added to the Mandatory Rotation section; (2) D0016 extension as friendly — gauntlet surfaced a canon MUST in P0006 (vodka boundary enumeration) which on inspection is itself status:proposed and scoped to MCP server specs (not P0001's category, so non-blocking near-miss), but the same challenge prompted a fetch of D0016 itself which revealed the genuine "exactly four properties / all other behavior is opaque" constraint; the "third eviction bound / friendly extension" framing was wrong and is now corrected to "composes ON TOP of D0016 via configuration discipline"; (3) pre-syndication-as-canonical — no hard tensions, P0001 already engages security-as-subscriber-pattern and D0020 explicitly. oddkit_audit on the new files returned 0 findings 0 files scanned because the branch is not yet pushed to GitHub (audit reads from GitHub state); manual python URI extraction over the 3 new files found 15/15 real ams:// URIs resolving (the 1 "miss" was a PNNNN- template placeholder in the governance doc, false positive). P0006 acknowledgment: spirit honored by P0001's existing "What This Proposes / Does Not Propose" sections even though the letter doesn't apply to a wrapper-primitive proposal.

content: Per project memory and prior session journals, both the GitHub PAT (klappy/klappy.dev + klappy/oddkit, scoped to contents + PRs) and the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY embedded in the project instructions have been flagged as needing rotation across multiple sessions. P0001's work used the existing PAT to clone and stage. No rotation occurred in this session. Standing open item — independent of the encryption proposal but worth re-flagging because the proposal's framing centers on key hygiene and the pre-existing condition is the strongest possible argument for the discipline P0001 codifies.

title: P0001 substantially upgraded — Threat Model + Mandatory Rotation sections added, frame format gains key_version byte, forward-secrecy bullet rewritten, tokens-as-arbitrary-entities clarification integrated throughout
content: Edits applied as 9 str_replace operations against canon/proposals/P0001-stream-encryption-as-pre-syndication-wrapper.md on branch claude/proposals-track-and-P0001-encryption-3K7m: (1) frontmatter derives_from extended to capture both corrections; (2) Description blurb mentions mandatory rotation as primitive-level property; (3) Outline gains Threat Model and Mandatory Rotation sections; (4) Threat Model section inserted between The Layering and Cardinality, naming patient adversary and training-corpus exfiltration in scope, traffic analysis and endpoint compromise out of scope; (5) Cardinality section rewritten with tokens-as-arbitrary-entities discipline and the generalized cardinality constraint for future primitives; (6) v1 Symmetric Primitive frame format updated to key_version || nonce || ciphertext || auth_tag (29 bytes overhead, AAD includes key_version), nonce discipline scoped to (stream, key_version), forward-secrecy bullet rewritten; (7) Mandatory Rotation section inserted with triggers (time + volume + event), set_metadata-based mechanism per PROTOCOL.md §4.4, and buffer composition framed as "compose ON TOP of D0016 via configuration discipline (rotation cadence ≤ buffer TTL)" — this corrected framing replaced the gauntlet-flagged false claim that key-version became a third eviction bound on D0016, which would have modified D0016's exactly-four-property primitive shape; (8) What This Proposes adds mandatory rotation, named threat model, tokens-as-arbitrary-entities discipline; (9) What This Does Not Propose forward-secrecy bullet replaced with per-frame-forward-secrecy-still-deferred. Status remains: proposed; the dial-tone-vs-application open question is unchanged.
